mentions.note: Value-oriented apparel/home goods retailer; reports tomorrow; tangible product focus; attractive rotation candidate.
Had a terrific quarter with HomeGoods, Marshalls performing well; stock hit despite good results but fundamentals remain strong.
Trade-down retail theme; HomeGoods, Marshalls, TJ Maxx divisions benefit as consumer shifts down.
MVP brand; everybody loves bargain business model. Value-for-price offering drives faster growth and multiple expansion.
Cleaning up via scavenger strategy on distressed retail inventory; extraordinary numbers across T.J. Maxx, Marshalls, HomeGoods.
Off-price retailer down 2.6%; had been phenomenal performer; decline paints nasty consumer picture.
Superb in weakening consumer environment; owned by Chapel Trust for years; can get merchandise from overburdened retailers at pennies on dollar; perfect inventory cleanout play.
6% SSS (vs 4.1% expected); 9% revenue growth; HomeGoods +9%; 29% earnings growth; raised guidance and buyback; off-price kingpin thriving as consumers trade down in K-shaped recovery.

Discount retail model based on selection (not price) winning while pure dollar stores lose luster; benefits from full-price retailer inventory liquidations; fundamentally different business model driving divergence.
Ideal off-price retailer for value shopping; held up very nicely today; defensive consumer play; own it.
Prime beneficiary of consumer trade-down; down 20 points from highs. Dual thesis: trade-down shoppers + excess inventory scooped at pennies on dollar from struggling peers.
